skill.name: ambiguous-question-guide
skill.description: 判断政务服务问题是否模糊，识别缺失要素（主语、证照名称、具体环节），生成精准的政务服务引导语。当用户提出不完整的办事问题或需要引导用户提供更多信息时使用。
skill.summary: Identifies ambiguous government service questions and generates precise clarification prompts for missing information.
skill.author: dxy-hub

security_audit.summary: All 173 static security findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' patterns are Markdown code block delimiters used for documentation examples, not actual shell execution. High entropy readings are normal for UTF-8 encoded Chinese text. This is a pure documentation skill with no executable code or security risks.

content.user_title: Clarify Ambiguous Questions for Government Services
content.value_statement: This skill helps identify when user questions about government services lack specific details. It guides users to provide missing information like document types, applicant categories, or specific processing stages.
actual_capabilities: Identifies missing key elements in user questions (subject, document name, specific stage)
Determines question ambiguity based on precision of business object description
Generates single, precise clarification questions following government service etiquette
Prioritizes missing elements: document name > subject > specific stage
Provides contextual examples to help users understand what information is needed
Distinguishes between ambiguous questions requiring clarification and specific questions ready for processing
limitations: Designed specifically for Chinese government service contexts
Requires user questions to contain a business object (what service/document they need)
Does not process or answer the actual service questions, only clarifies them
Relies on predefined judgment rules and may not cover all edge cases

use_cases.title: Government Service Chatbot
use_cases.description: A government agency uses this skill in their chatbot to detect when citizens ask incomplete questions about permits, certificates, or policies, and automatically ask for missing details.

use_cases.title: Customer Support Triage
use_cases.description: A customer support system uses this skill to identify when user inquiries lack sufficient information and automatically prompt for clarification before routing to human agents.

use_cases.title: Question Analysis Training
use_cases.description: A training program for government service representatives uses this skill to teach staff how to recognize ambiguous questions and ask precise follow-up questions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Ambiguity Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this user question about government services: '{user_question}'. Identify if it contains a specific business object (document type, service name, or policy). If the question is ambiguous and lacks specific details like document name, applicant type, or processing stage, output '0'. If the question is specific enough to search for procedures, output '1'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick binary classification to determine if a question needs clarification

prompt_templates.title: Generate Clarification Question
prompt_templates.prompt: User asked: '{user_question}'. This question is ambiguous because it lacks {missing_element}. Generate a single, polite clarification question following this format: '请问您{verb}{missing_element}呢？'. Optionally add examples in parentheses like '（如：{example1}、{example2}等）'. Output only the question, no explanation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a precise follow-up question when the missing element is identified

prompt_templates.title: Complete Analysis and Response
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this user question: '{user_question}'. First, determine if it's ambiguous by checking for: 1) standard business name or professional terminology, 2) 'specific qualifier + generic term' structure, 3) vague description or generic terms only. If ambiguous, identify the highest priority missing element (document name > subject > specific stage) and generate one clarification question. If specific, mark for direct processing.
prompt_templates.scenario: End-to-end handling of user inquiries with automatic clarification generation

prompt_templates.title: Batch Question Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: For each question in this list, determine: 1) Is it ambiguous (output 0 or 1)? 2) If ambiguous, what is the missing element? 3) Generate the appropriate clarification question. Questions: {question_list}. Format output as a table with columns: Question, Ambiguous (0/1), Missing Element, Clarification Question (if applicable).
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing multiple questions at once for analysis or training purposes

output_examples.input: User asks: '我要办证' (I want to get a certificate)
output_examples.output: 请问您需要办理的是什么类型的证件呢？

output_examples.input: User asks: '食品经营许可证怎么办' (How do I get a food business license?)
output_examples.output: 请问您是首次申请还是变更/延续呢？

output_examples.input: User asks: '营业执照需要什么材料' (What documents do I need for a business license?)
output_examples.output: 请问您是个体工商户还是企业办理呢？

best_practices: Always ask only one question at a time, focusing on the highest priority missing element
Use polite government service language starting with '请问' and ending with '呢？'
Provide contextual examples in parentheses when the missing element could refer to multiple options
anti_patterns: Asking multiple questions at once, which overwhelms users and violates the single-question principle
Using generic prompts like '请详细说明您的需求' (Please explain your needs in detail) that don't guide users effectively
Outputting explanations or reasoning alongside the clarification question, when only the question should be displayed

faq.question: What types of questions can this skill analyze?
faq.answer: This skill analyzes questions about government services including permits, certificates, policies, and administrative procedures. It works best with questions in Chinese related to business licenses, identity documents, subsidies, and other government services.

faq.question: How does the skill determine if a question is ambiguous?
faq.answer: The skill checks if the question contains a standard business name, professional terminology, or a 'specific qualifier + generic term' structure. If the question only provides a scenario description or uses generic terms without qualifiers, it is classified as ambiguous.

faq.question: What is the priority order for identifying missing elements?
faq.answer: The priority order is: document name (highest priority), subject (medium priority), and specific stage (lower priority). For example, if a question lacks both document name and subject, the skill will ask about the document name first.

faq.question: Can this skill be used for languages other than Chinese?
faq.answer: This skill is specifically designed for Chinese government service contexts and uses Chinese language patterns. It would need significant modification to work effectively with other languages or different cultural contexts.

faq.question: Does this skill answer the actual service questions?
faq.answer: No, this skill only identifies when questions are unclear and generates clarification prompts. Once the user provides sufficient information, a separate system or process would handle the actual service inquiry.

faq.question: What should I do if the skill misclassifies a question?
faq.answer: The skill relies on predefined patterns and may not handle all edge cases. You can refine the judgment rules in the references/judgment-rules.md file or add special case handling to improve accuracy for your specific use cases.
